Title: News@9: Today's top headlines - June 16, 2026 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Here are today's biggest stories.
1MDB judgment released
The full judgment in Datuk Seri Najib Razak's 1MDB case was released today, describing the scandal as a financial plunder of staggering proportions.
Ikram member charged
A Pertubuhan Ikram Malaysia member has begun facing trial on 158 abuse of power charges involving alleged gratification.
Anti-bullying law begins
The Anti-Bullying Act has come into force, alongside the launch of the Anti-Bullying Tribunal to handle cases involving minors.
PH welcomes competition
Datuk Fahmi Fadzil says Pakatan Harapan should view the emergence of new political parties as an opportunity to strengthen its efforts ahead of state elections.
That's it for News@9.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'He vanished from sight': Search continues for missing angler who fell off boat
Article:
KUANTAN: An angler attempted to reach out to his friend, who was struggling in the water, before he
disappeared beneath the surface
in a tragic incident at Sungai Kuantan near Kampung Padang here today.
Badrul Afiq Jailani, 24, said he panicked when Muhammad Firdaus Zuharsema, 26, who was on the boat with him, fell into the river after their boat collided with floating driftwood about 9.20am.
"We had gone to cast our fishing nets and while returning to the jetty, the boat hit the driftwood. Firdaus fell into the water and I lost control of the boat, causing it to spin in circles.
"After bringing the boat under control, I rushed towards Firdaus to grab his hands, but he vanished from sight. I screamed for help when another angler nearby contacted the authorities," he told reporters today.
Badrul Afiq said both of them had been close friends since childhood, and Firdaus, who held a full-time job, would usually join him to go fishing on his days off.
Kuantan police chief Ashari Abu Samah said the incident occurred about 1.5km from the jetty along the river.
He said the search and rescue operation, involving personnel from the Civil Defence Force, firefighters and police, is still ongoing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Judge: Najib, Jho Low worked 'hand-in-hand' to siphon 1MDB funds
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Najib Razak was in cahoots with fugitive businessman Low Taek Jho to siphon billions of ringgit in 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB) funds.
Judge Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah said the then prime minister orchestrated the global-scale financial scheme through wiles and deception of Low, also known as Jho Low, as his proxy.
"They worked hand-in-hand to carefully and meticulously fashion a complex web of deceit through a series of transactions that resulted in funds being siphoned from 1MDB.
"It was done through multiple layers of transfers, eventually finding their ways into the accused' private bank accounts.
"The evidence clearly points to the fact that this was no coincidence but was indicative of a relationship in which Low operated as a proxy or agent of the accused in relation to the management of 1MDB's affairs.
"The attempt by the accused to distance himself from Low rang hollow in light of the overwhelming evidence adduced by these prosecution witnesses," he said.
Sequerah, who is now a Federal Court judge, said this in his 810-page grounds of judgment in the 1MDB case involving Najib where he was found guilty of all abuse of power and money laundering charges.
He added that Najib's argument that he was too busy running the country to oversee 1MDB operations was also not acceptable.
Najib was sentenced to 15 years in jail and fined RM13.4 billion after he was found guilty of all counts of abuse of power and 21 money laundering charges involving RM2.28 billion in 1MDB funds on Dec 26, last year.
Najib will face an additional 40 years in prison if he fails to settle the fine.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Judge: 1MDB money transferred into Najib's personal account 'not chicken feed'
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Najib Razak's failure to act over the transfer of billions of ringgit linked to the 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB) funds into his personal bank accounts was unreasonable.
Judge Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah said the sums deposited into the then prime minister's AmBank account, including US$681 million, were not small amounts and warranted a reasonable response.
"This was not chicken feed. The sums remitted were staggering in its enormity," he said in his 810-page grounds of judgment in the 1MDB case involving Najib where he was found guilty of all abuse of power and money laundering charges.
Sequerah said Najib himself testified that he did not verify how Tanore Finance Corporation obtained his account details and did not conduct any investigation into the transfers.
It was revealed during the trial that Tanore was a shell company controlled by fugitive businessman Low Taek Jho, better known as Jho Low.
Sequerah said Najib's reaction was inconsistent with that of a reasonable man.
He said Najib was not "blind" to the misappropriation of 1MDB funds, but had taken steps to protect his own interests.
These included the dissolution of the 1MDB Special Task Force in July 2015, the removal of then attorney-general Tan Sri Abdul Gani Patail, and the ousting of Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission chief commissioner Tan Sri Abu Kassim Mohamed.
Sequerah said Najib also attempted to persuade then Bank Negara governor Tan Sri Zeti Akhtar Aziz to issue a statement clearing the billions credited into his account.
"All of the above shows more than a passing interest in 1MDB affairs and the extent of measures taken to protect and reinforce those interests," he said.
The court further found that Najib's return of funds to the Saudi royal family was a "pointer" that the arrangement was structured to ensure he continued benefiting from it, despite claims of an "Arab donation."
It noted inconsistencies in his testimony, which shifted between claims that the funds came from the late King Abdullah, the Saudi royal family, and Prince Saud, concluding that the return of funds was arranged to disguise the origin while maintaining benefit to the accused.
The court also said the absence of police reports against Low over alleged instructions not originating from Najib indicated that the rogue businessman was not acting independently in deceiving Najib.
Najib was sentenced to 15 years in jail and fined RM13.4 billion after he was found guilty of all counts of abuse of power and 21 money laundering charges involving RM2.28 billion in 1MDB funds on Dec 26, last year.
Najib will face an additional 40 years in prison if he fails to settle the fine.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral: Lorry driver saves lives by using vehicle to block runaway bus [WATCH]
Article:
IPOH: A multi-vehicle pile up was narrowly averted along the North-South Expressway when a quick-thinking lorry driver used the rear of his vehicle to absorb the impact of a runaway express bus.
It is believed that the incident occurred along the southbound stretch at Km265 just past the notorious Menora tunnel slope, and was captured on a dashcam.
The original 24-second footage was initially uploaded to Facebook by user Tan Sri Noor Azam and was subsequently shared to Tragedi Terkini Hari Ini (2026), by user Puteri Intan Surfina.
In the clip, the express bus appears to be out of control while descending the hill and hitting the guardrail. It also was seen narrowly missing several other smaller passenger vehicles.
Sensing the urgency of the situation, the driver of a nearby lorry ahead positioned his vehicle directly in front of the bus.
He then allowed the bus to safely slam into his vehicle's rear end, using his own brakes to slow down and eventually bring the bus to a complete stop.
Netizens have flooded the comment sections across social platforms, hailing the unnamed lorry driver as a hero.
The New Straits Times has reached out to the authorities in Ipoh for confirmation of the incident.
User Puteri said: "The incident happened after the Menora Tunnel. Luckily, that lorry was there to act as a barrier and stop the bus. The lorry driver did an absolutely brilliant job.
"The brakes failed, which is why this is something that needs to be checked constantly."
Another user, Asri Yeop, said: "The lorry driver took or was forced to bear a huge risk; that metal divider could have killed him. The bus company should absolutely pay for his damages and give him a reward."
User RedPanda5771, meanwhile, called the lorry driver a hero and suggested he be given a medal.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Energy firms brace for 'new era' despite Hormuz deal
Article:
PARIS: Oil and gas majors have high hopes for a quick reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, but they have few illusions about a return to normal for the Gulf energy industry after more than three months of blockage.
Even if the deal between Iran and the United States to end the Mideast war holds, analysts say the old market certainties are gone for good -- and the new risks will probably require costly adaptions.
Business as usual?
"A credible reopening of the Strait of Hormuz would be one of the most important developments for the global economy at this juncture," said Claudio Galimberti, chief economist at Rystad Energy.
The shock of a near-doubling of oil prices since Iran effectively closed off the crucial waterway has fuelled inflation that could persist for months, threatening growth around the globe.
Its closure exposed the vulnerability of Gulf supply chains -- and now the prospect of Iranian tolls on tanker and cargo traffic.
Iran's foreign ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei has evoked maritime "service fees" on ships transiting the strait, which companies are likely to pass on to customers.
"We're in a whole new era," French expert Philippe Chalmin of the raw materials consulting group Cyclope said this month in presenting its annual market outlook, an industry reference.
"Hormuz can be blocked -- no one had imagined that," he said, noting that a toll was likely.
Energy firms appear to have resigned themselves to the fact, with TotalEnergies chief Patrick Pouyanne saying in April that "compensation" to Iran is preferable to having the strait stay closed.
What's the immediate impact?
Oil prices fell sharply after the deal was announced to around $80 a barrel, though that remains above the mid-$60s seen before the war.
"We're really waiting for the signature of a concrete agreement and tangible proof that the Strait of Hormuz is reopening," said Blandine Ruty, secretary general of the French petroleum industry association Ufipem.
She predicted that prices would fall further "if there indeed are signs of political stability".
However, if market sentiment has clearly improved, "sentiment is not the same as supply", said Rystad's Galimberti.
Retaliatory strikes by Iran have targeted oil and gas infrastructure in several Gulf countries seen helping the US attacks, taking many pumps and refineries offline.
"It will take time for production to ramp back up, for logistics to normalise, and for the risk premium embedded in crude prices to dissipate," he said.
He also noted the United Arab Emirates' decision during the war to quit OPEC, hindering the Saudi-dominated cartel's ability to control oil prices.
Chalmin at Cyclope meanwhile said "there are doubts that some wells will be able to quickly start flowing again -- It's not just a tap you turn back on".
How will the industry adapt?
"This war showed the oil and gas industry that Hormuz risk is no longer just a geopolitical headline. It is a logistics, insurance, storage and balance sheet risk," said Stephen Innes, an analyst at SPI Asset Management.
He predicted the industry would shift from "just-in-time" to "just-in-case" logistics that mean "more storage, more flexible shipping, more diversified crude and LNG sourcing, and more planning around war-risk insurance".
The UAE has already said it will fast-track construction of a new oil pipeline bypassing the Strait of Hormuz by reaching the port of Fujairah on the Gulf of Oman.
But that marginally eases shipping risks only for crude oil, given the overwhelming importance of tanker shipments -- Liquefied natural gas (LNG) exports require ships for reaching key Asian and European markets.
"For refined products and LNG, if the Strait of Hormuz is blocked, you're blocked 100 per cent," said Francis Perrin of the French Institute for International and Strategic Affairs (IRIS), a contributor to Cyclope's annual report.
Even if producers build more "resilience" into their operations, it will be reflected in their costs.
"The industry should rethink storage, pipelines, production diversity and transit routes, but only within realistic limits," Innes said.
"You can build buffers, but you cannot build another Hormuz overnight."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Teacher acquitted of outraging modesty charge
Article:
BUTTERWORTH: A teacher was acquitted of an outraging modesty charge after the prosecution failed to establish a prima facie case against him.
Magistrate Aini Adillah Muhammad Faizal today acquitted and discharged Muhammad Irfan Fathullah Mohammad Farizuan, 25, without calling for his defence at the Butterworth magistrate's court here.
In delivering her ruling, Aini Adillah held that, upon a maximum evaluation of the prosecution's evidence, they had failed to prove a prima facie case against the accused.
She further ruled that the delay in lodging the police report and material contradictions between the complainant's police report and her complaint letter raised doubts over the occurrence of the alleged offence.
"The accused is entitled to the benefit of the doubt, which warrants his acquittal without being called to enter his defence," she said.
Muhammad Irfan, who was a Grade DG9 Education Service Officer attached to a Chinese primary school at the time of the alleged offence, had been charged under Section 354 of the Penal Code.
He was accused of using criminal force against a 25-year-old woman by allegedly touching her breast with intent to outrage her modesty along the Butterworth Outer Ring Road (BORR) here about 1pm in February 2022.
The offence carries a punishment of up to 10 years' imprisonment, a fine, whipping, or any two of the punishments upon conviction.
The prosecution was conducted by Deputy public prosecutor Lim Zhan Yii, while the accused was represented by lawyer Sagthiyaraj Selvarajoo.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Najib never showed remorse for 1MDB scandal, says court
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak did not show any remorse over his role in the 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB) scandal, instead shifting blame to other parties.
Judge Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah said the absence of remorse was a key aggravating factor in sentencing, noting that public anger over the misappropriation must be reflected in punishment given the scale of the offence and its damage to Malaysia's reputation.
The judge said Najib's 47 years of public service, including as prime minister, was considered a mitigating factor, but stressed the issue before the court was whether it outweighed a serious breach of trust at the highest level of government.
"The public anger (over the misappropriation) should be reflected in the sentence imposed, given the scale of the offences and the negative reputation it has brought to the country.
"The court must also duly consider that the accused did not show or exhibit any remorse for the offences committed, instead choosing to place the blame for this scandal on other parties, including the management of 1MDB.
"The absence of remorse is a highly relevant factor to be taken into account in sentencing," he said.
He said this in his 810-page grounds of judgment in the 1MDB case involving Najib where he was found guilty of all abuse of power and money laundering charges.
Sequerah said the 1MDB scandal involved liabilities of nearly RM50 billion, with about RM42 billion already borne by the government, while some RM13 billion remains outstanding despite RM29 billion recovered.
"It cannot be denied that the financial scandal has reverberated across the world. Its effects and consequences are still ongoing, and there are continuing financial obligations that must be met, which will also affect future generations," he said.
He said the case was not one of an ordinary employee exploiting systemic weaknesses, but of a person in the highest office who abused his position to influence decisions involving the 1MDB board.
"The abuse and betrayal of trust must be a primary consideration in sentencing," he said.
The court also noted that Najib was listed in criminal records based on official Malaysian and Singapore registries and checks by the Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department.
In delivering sentence considerations, Sequerah said a deterrent sentence was warranted given the magnitude of the offences and their long-term impact, adding that public interest is served when punishment deters future wrongdoing and encourages reform.
He added that whether an offender truly reforms is ultimately a matter only time can determine.
"Whether a person truly becomes better after time has passed, through reflection on past wrongdoing and the process of rehabilitation, is something that only time will tell," he added.
Najib was sentenced to 15 years in jail and fined RM13.4 billion after he was found guilty of all counts of abuse of power and 21 money laundering charges involving RM2.28 billion in 1MDB funds on Dec 26, last year.
Najib will face an additional 40 years in prison if he fails to settle the fine.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar's Kazan visit to strengthen Malaysia-Russia, Asean-Russia ties - Envoy
Article:
KAZAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is scheduled to meet Rais of the Republic of Tatarstan Rustam Minnikhanov during his two-day working visit to Kazan beginning June 17.
He will also deliver a keynote address at the Asean-Russia Business Forum, attend a reception hosted by Russian President Vladimir Putin, and hold bilateral meetings with Russian leaders.
Malaysia's Ambassador to Russia Datuk Cheong Loon Lai said discussions during the visit are expected to focus on efforts to expand cooperation in trade and investment, energy, food security, the digital economy, education, culture and tourism.
"Malaysia hopes this visit will open up more opportunities for new cooperation and further strengthen the strategic relationship that has been established between Malaysia and Russia, while also contributing to the overall strengthening of Asean-Russia relations," he told the media ahead of Anwar's arrival in Kazan tonight.
Anwar will be in Kazan to attend the 35th Asean-Russia Commemorative Summit, which will be held on June 17 and 18.
Also joining the Malaysian delegation are Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani and Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir.
Cheong said Anwar is scheduled to meet Minnikhanov shortly after arriving in Kazan tonight to discuss efforts to strengthen existing cooperation between Malaysia and the republic.
"The two leaders, who have met several times over the past two years, are expected to continue discussions on cooperation in trade, investment and civilisational dialogue on Islamic thought through the Special Vision Group (SVG) Russia and the Islamic World.
"On June 17, Anwar is scheduled to deliver a keynote address at the Asean-Russia Business Forum, which will bring together more than 400 corporate and business leaders from Russia and Asean member states," he said.
Anwar is also scheduled to participate in an exclusive interview with Russia Today News before attending a reception in conjunction with the 35th Asean-Russia Summit, hosted by Putin and attended by Asean leaders.
The Prime Minister will also pay a courtesy call on Putin, followed by a bilateral meeting to discuss Malaysia-Russia strategic cooperation, including efforts to deepen, enhance and explore new areas of collaboration, as well as seek solutions to issues and challenges faced by both countries.
According to Cheong, on June 18, Asean and Russia will commemorate the 35th anniversary of Asean-Russia dialogue relations through the 35th Asean-Russia Commemorative Summit and a working lunch involving Asean and Russian leaders.
"In addition, the leaders are expected to exchange views on regional and international developments of mutual interest. This exchange will provide an opportunity for both sides to strengthen mutual understanding and explore new areas of cooperation that can contribute to regional peace, stability and prosperity," he said.
The summit is expected to adopt four outcome documents, namely the Kazan Declaration on the 35th Anniversary of Asean-Russia Relations, the Joint Statement on Energy Cooperation, the Joint Statement on Cultural Cooperation, and the Comprehensive Plan of Action to Implement the Asean-Russia Strategic Partnership 2026-2030.
These documents will serve as important guides for strengthening future cooperation and building a more resilient and forward-looking Asean-Russia partnership.
When asked whether the issue of purchasing oil and gas from Russia would be discussed, Cheong said he believed the matter would be raised during the meeting.
"This is something that I believe will be linked to these discussions because I think it is one of the key issues at the forefront of the talks. I believe it will be discussed," he said, referring to the Prime Minister's earlier remarks on the matter.
Anwar was recently reported as saying that ensuring the country's energy supply remains a key government priority as he undertakes a working visit to Kazan for important meetings with Putin.
He said the visit, which includes meetings between Asean leaders and the Russian leadership, would be utilised to ensure the continuity and security of Malaysia's oil supply.
In 2024, total Asean-Russia trade amounted to USD18.1 billion, while Russian foreign direct investment in Asean was recorded at RM367.90 million (USD92.97 million).
For Malaysia, Russia was the country's ninth-largest trading partner among European nations in 2025, with total trade valued at RM8.72 billion (USD2.04 billion).
Malaysia's main exports to Russia comprise electrical and electronic products, machinery, equipment and parts, as well as processed food products, while its principal imports from Russia include petroleum products, minerals, chemicals and chemical-based products.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AI optical parts maker Zhongji Innolight eyes up to US$7bil Hong Kong listing
Article:
SINGAPORE/HONG KONG: Chinese optical parts maker Zhongji Innolight is planning to launch a share listing in Hong Kong as early as mid-July that could raise up to US$7 billion, two sources with direct knowledge of the matter said.
The Shenzhen-listed company, which makes optical modules used in AI data centres, initially aimed to raise about US$5 billion, but the target has since risen to about US$7 billion after strong investor interest during roadshows, one of the people said.
Zhongji Innolight hopes to receive clearance from Chinese regulators for the second listing by late June, one of the people said. Chinese companies need to complete a listing filing with the China Securities Regulatory Commission before they can sell shares offshore.F
The company has confidentially filed for the listing, both sources said.
All the sources declined to be named as the matter is private.
Zhongji Innolight did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
A listing of that size would be Hong Kong's biggest this year and the largest Chinese technology listing in the city in at least the past 12 months, according to LSEG data as of June 11.
It would be more than twice the size of Victory Giant Technology (Huizhou)'s US$2.73 billion Hong Kong second listing in April, currently the biggest Chinese tech share sale in the city over that period, the data showed.
TECHNOLOGY LISTINGS BOOST FOR HONG KONG
The planned listing would help support the revival in Hong Kong's market for Chinese technology share sales.
Chinese technology IPOs and second listings in Hong Kong have raised US$11.24 billion so far this year, up from US$235.6 million in the same period last year, according to LSEG.
Separately, Shenzhen-listed electronics manufacturer and Apple supplier Luxshare Precision Industry Co is preparing investor education for a listing in Hong Kong that could raise about US$3 billion, according to the first source who has direct knowledge of the matters. It could launch as early as July, the source said.
Luxshare did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
The company said on June 12 it had received CSRC filing confirmation for a planned Hong Kong listing of up to 441 million shares.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Uzbekistan keen to deepen investment, business links with Malaysia, Asean
Article:
TASHKENT: Uzbekistan is keen to strengthen investment and business ties with Malaysia and other Asean countries, particularly in high-value-added industries, manufacturing and small and medium enterprises (SMEs).
Adviser to the Ministry of Economy and Finance Jasur Karshibayev said Uzbekistan remains open to investors from all countries and sees significant potential to expand economic cooperation with Malaysia, underpinned by strong cultural ties and growing engagement between the two nations.
"Asean countries are another area where we are happy to collaborate. Uzbekistan is open for business from all countries, including Malaysia," he told Bernama on the sidelines of the Tashkent International Investment Forum (TIIF) 2026 here today.
Earlier this month, Uzbekistan Ambassador to Malaysia Karomidin Gadoyev said Malaysian investments in Uzbekistan have surpassed US$3 billion (US$1 = RM4.06), underscoring deepening economic ties between the two countries.
He highlighted that the number of Malaysia-Uzbekistan joint ventures rose to 47, underscoring growing business engagement between the two countries.
Karshibayev said Kuala Lumpur and Tashkent could emerge as stronger business partners as both countries explore new trade and investment opportunities.
He added that Uzbekistan has identified a pipeline of priority projects, including privatisation initiatives and investments across various sectors, with particular interest in industries that generate higher value-added economic activities.
Karshibayev noted that while governments can facilitate investment by providing the necessary framework and policy support, meaningful collaboration ultimately depends on private sector participation.
Regarding the development of the SME sector, he added that it remains a key government priority due to its role in job creation and economic growth, and that the country is open to learning from Malaysia's experience in supporting SME development and attracting greater private-sector participation in the segment.
Meanwhile, Karshibayev said Uzbekistan remains confident of achieving its investment ambitions under the Uzbekistan 2030 strategy, which targets attracting US$250 billion in investments.
Karshibayev said the government would continue implementing reforms to improve the investment climate and attract more private capital into the economy.
Since 2017, Uzbekistan has launched wide-ranging reforms aimed at modernising the economy, improving the business environment, and attracting sustainable investment.
Key achievements include the liberalisation of the foreign exchange regime, the adoption of modern investment legislation, judicial reforms, and enhanced e-government services.
Karshibayev said the government's reform agenda has so far yielded positive results, pointing to easing inflation despite extensive price liberalisation measures and continued economic growth.
He added that Uzbekistan has also maintained strong economic momentum despite global uncertainties, reflecting the effectiveness of ongoing reforms.
Earlier, Karshibayev was one of the panellists at a session on "Political Risk and Blended Finance: Mobilising Capital For Frontier Markets".
He noted that the Uzbek government aims to increase the country's economy to US$240 billion by 2030, thereby joining the ranks of upper-middle-income countries.
To achieve this goal, Kashibayev said the Uzbek government plans to continue economic and social reforms while further improving the business environment.
In addition, it was noted that Uzbekistan's sovereign credit ratings have been consistently improving in recent years, which is expected to reduce the cost of raising capital in international markets.
Franklin Templeton's chief executive officer for Central Asia, Marius Dan, said the recently completed National Investment Fund IPO comprised 13 strategically important state-owned enterprises and was successfully conducted through dual listings in London and Tashkent.
He said the transaction marked Uzbekistan's first-ever international equity offering and raised close to US$700 million, with investors including major international financial institutions such as BlackRock, Franklin Resources, and Redwheel.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Vantris Energy to shed PN17 status on Thursday
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Vantris Energy Bhd, formerly known as Sapura Energy Bhd, is set to be uplifted from its Practice Note 17 (PN17) status on Thursday.
The upliftment of the company's PN17 status will take effect at 9am on June 18, according to a Bursa Malaysia filing.
The company said Bursa Malaysia's approval of its application for the upliftment marks a major milestone in its recovery journey.
This follows the successful completion of its financial restructuring efforts and its achievement of two consecutive quarters of profitability.
On Monday, Vantris Energy posted a net profit of RM145.79 million for the first quarter of the financial year ending Jan 31, 2027, compared with a net loss of RM477.95 million recorded in the same quarter a year earlier.
Its chief executive officer Muhammad Zamri Jusoh said the upliftment of the PN17 status provides a sense of relief.
It also recognises the dedication, perseverance and confidence of those who supported the company throughout a particularly challenging period.
"We are grateful to Bursa Malaysia, regulators, shareholders, financiers, vendors, clients, our employees and all stakeholders who continued to support the group through our restructuring journey.
"Their trust and confidence gave us the opportunity to rebuild. The approval represents an important step forward, but does not mark the end of the group's recovery journey," he said in a statement.
Vantris Energy was classified as a PN17 company in May 2022 following a period of significant financial distress.
The company and its subsidiaries subsequently carried out an extensive corporate restructuring programme, which included debt reorganisation, capital restructuring, fresh investments, creditor settlements and measures to strengthen its financial standing.
Vantris Energy successfully completed its regularisation plan and reached the restructuring effective date on Sept 26, 2025.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fahmi: Malaysia, UK have spent two years in talks over social media regulation
Article:
KOTA BARU: Malaysia's discussions with the United Kingdom on regulating social media platforms, including proposals to restrict access for users under the age of 16, have been ongoing for more than two years.
Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the engagement involves continuous consultations between Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) and the United Kingdom's communications regulator, Ofcom.
Fahmi said Ofcom had, at the early stage of discussions, viewed Malaysia's proposal to introduce a licensing framework for social media platforms through MCMC as a positive initiative.
"Discussions with Ofcom have indeed been ongoing, and we also welcome the announcement made by the UK prime minister," he said.
He was speaking to reporters after presenting appointment letters to chairmen of the National Information Dissemination Centre (Nadi) state advisory panels.
Fahmi said the latest developments in the UK reflected a growing global trend towards tighter regulation of social media usage among children.
"The action taken by the UK clearly shows that more countries are now making similar decisions and adopting similar approaches.
"This development also proves that social media platforms, in general, are not safe for children under the age of 16," he said.
Meanwhile, Fahmi said the Communications Ministry, through MCMC, is rolling out a nationwide "Internet Selamat" campaign to ensure communities, especially children, are aware of online safety issues.
He said the initiative includes raising awareness that users under 16 are not allowed to open social media accounts, as well as strengthening education on the risks of cyberbullying, scams, and online gambling.
Fahmi said the campaign will be implemented by leveraging the Community Madani network across Kelantan, together with chairmen of Nadi advisory panels, to jointly promote safe and responsible internet usage among the public.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit closes lower against greenback on BOJ interest rate increase
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit closed lower against the US dollar on Tuesday as investors remained cautious following the Bank of Japan's (BOJ) decision to raise interest rates.
At 6pm, the local note fell to 4.0665/0700 versus the greenback from 4.0485/0525 at Monday's close.
Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the BOJ's decision to raise its policy rate prompted investors to remain cautious despite declining crude oil prices.
The Japanese central bank increased its policy rate by 25 basis points to 1.00 per cent, as widely expected, marking the highest level since 1995, he noted.
"The decision was premised on rising inflation and improving economic growth conditions, which make the extremely accommodative monetary policy increasingly inappropriate," Mohd Afzanizam told Bernama.
At the close, the ringgit was lower against a basket of major currencies.
It depreciated against the Japanese yen to 2.5360/5384 from 2.5283/5309 at Monday's close, weakened versus the British pound to 5.4552/4599 from 5.4355/4409 yesterday, and slipped vis-a-vis the euro to 4.7175/7216 from 4.6987/7033.
The local note also eased against regional currencies.
It weakened against the Thai baht to 12.5027/5192 from 12.4294/4474 at Monday's close, slid versus the Indonesian rupiah to 229.3/229.7 from 228.5/228.9 yesterday, dropped vis-a-vis the Philippine peso to 6.74/6.75 from 6.69/6.70, and declined against the Singapore dollar to 3.1722/1752 from 3.1580/1613.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bar Council Child Rights Committee lauds Anti-Bullying Tribunal
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The launch of the Anti-Bullying Tribunal has been lauded as the best mechanism to immediately address the rise in bullying cases among minors in the country.
Bar Council Child Rights Committee co-chairman MK Thas said the new legal framework, which binds the families of perpetrators under the age of 18 to awards issued by the tribunal, is a vital step toward enforcing joint accountability.
"Under the circumstances, this would be the best mechanism to move forward.
"This process reflects accountability, and this will make the tribunal far more effective in protecting our children," he told the
New Straits Times
.
The Anti-Bully Act 2026, aimed at resolving bullying disputes involving minors, officially
came into force
today alongside the launch of the Anti-Bullying Tribunal.
Thas noted that the implementation of the tribunal will instill greater confidence among parents, assuring them that such matters will be handled with the utmost seriousness.
He added that with these legal mechanisms in place, any early signs or elements of bullying detected by parents can be addressed and eliminated before it escalates.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar to lead M'sian delegation to Asean-Russia commemorative summit in Kazan
Article:
KAZAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will lead the Malaysian delegation to the Asean-Russia Commemorative Summit in Kazan from June 17 to 18.
In a statement today, Wisma Putra said Anwar will be accompanied by Minister of Investment, Trade and Industry Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani, Minister of Economy Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir as well as officials from the Prime Minister's Office and the Foreign Ministry.
The summit will mark the 35th anniversary of Asean-Russia relations, which were established in Kuala Lumpur in 1991.
According to the statement, the meeting will provide an opportunity for Asean and Russia to review progress achieved over the past 35 years and chart the future direction of cooperation under the Asean-Russia Strategic Partnership.
Discussions are expected to focus on strengthening practical cooperation in trade and investment, energy, food security, digital economy, science and technology, culture, education, tourism, and people-to-people exchanges.
Leaders are also expected to exchange views on regional and international developments.
The summit is expected to adopt four outcome documents, namely the Kazan Declaration on the 35th Anniversary of Asean-Russia Relations, the Joint Statement on Energy Cooperation, the Joint Statement on Cultural Cooperation, and the Comprehensive Plan of Action to Implement the Asean-Russia Strategic Partnership 2026-2030.
Wisma Putra said the documents will guide Asean-Russia cooperation in the coming years and support efforts to build a more resilient and future-oriented partnership.
"Malaysia's participation reflects the country's commitment to strengthening Asean's external relations, promoting Asean Centrality, and advancing practical cooperation with Dialogue Partners in an increasingly complex global environment," the statement said.
While in Kazan, Anwar is also expected to meet Russian President Vladimir Putin and the Rais of the Republic of Tatarstan, and hold engagements with Asean counterparts to discuss bilateral relations and issues of common concern.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Beshom unit to dispose of Kapar industrial land for RM85.8mil
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Beshom Holdings Bhd's wholly-owned subsidiary, Hai-O Enterprise Bhd, has entered into a sale and purchase agreement with JT Development Sdn Bhd for the proposed disposal of three plots of freehold industrial land for a cash consideration of RM85.80 million.
The land, measuring 58,994 square metres, is located along the main road of Jalan Kapar in Klang, Selangor, directly opposite the Grand Sepadu Highway toll plaza. The expressway provides access to North Port and Port Klang.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, the investment holding company said the proposed disposal represents an opportunity for Beshom Group to unlock and realise the value of the land, with a pro forma gain on disposal of about RM52.78 million.
"The proposed disposal will enable the group to have a cash inflow of RM85.80 million, which will be used for, among others, the acquisition of a new warehouse, the construction of a new TNB substation, the refurbishment of existing properties, and working capital for the expansion of retail stores.
"The proceeds will also be used for the refurbishment of existing retail stores, product development and supply chain sourcing, member development programmes, recognition and training activities, information technology system upgrades, and the subscription of new IT applications to enhance online marketing for the multi-level marketing, retail and wholesale segments," it said.
Beshom said the utilisation of the proceeds is expected to contribute positively to the group's operations.
The company said the proposed disposal is subject to, among others, approval from its shareholders at an extraordinary general meeting to be convened.
"Barring any unforeseen circumstances and subject to all required approvals being obtained, the board expects the proposed disposal to be completed in the first quarter of 2027," it said.
Affin Hwang Investment Bank has been appointed as the principal adviser for the proposed disposal.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MPB chairman Syed Hussian named Yayasan Tuanku Syed Putra Icon Entrepreneur
Article:
ARAU: The Raja of Perlis Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Putra Jamalullail has presented the Yayasan Tuanku Syed Putra (YTSP) Entrepreneur Icon Award to Media Prima Bhd group chairman Datuk Seri Dr Syed Hussian Aljunid.
The recognition was given in appreciation of Syed Hussian's contribution to Malaysia's economy and for successfully driving organisational growth and local industry transformation.
The award was presented during a ceremony held in conjunction with the 83rd birthday of the Raja of Perlis at Al-Madrasah Ad-Diniah Al-Islamiah today.
Also present at the event were Raja Muda Perlis Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra Jamalullail and the Raja Puan Muda Perlis Tuanku Dr Hajah Lailatul Shahreen Akashah Khalil.
Perlis-born Syed Hussian was recognised for his vast experience spanning over four decades across various sectors, including technology, media, insurance, and business development.
His leadership is reflected not only through outstanding business achievements, but also his continuous commitment to empowering communities and advancing the technology industry.
He is also committed to his role as the chairman of the board of trustees for Amanah Ikhtiar Malaysia (AIM), which works to empower low-income communities through microfinancing.
Before joining MPB, Syed Hussian served for more than 20 years with Western Digital, successfully expanding operations through foreign direct investments reaching RM18 billion.
His commitment is also evident in human capital development through his involvement in higher education, including serving as a board member of Universiti Malaysia Sarawak, and as an adjunct professor at Universiti Tenaga Nasional and Multimedia University.
The award reflects the appreciation for his continuous contributions to advancing industries, empowering entrepreneurship, and contributing to the nation's socio-economic development.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Scanwolf secures RM75mil substructure project in KL
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Scanwolf Corporation Bhd (SCB) has secured a RM75 million contract for piling and substructure works related to a 74-storey mixed development project in Jalan Pavilion, Kuala Lumpur.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, the company said its wholly owned subsidiary Scanwolf Trading & Construction Sdn Bhd accepted the letter of award (LOA) from Hallson Construction Sdn Bhd.
The project is undertaken for Tetuan Armani Hallson Sdn Bhd.
Scheduled to commence today, June 16, 2026, it is expected to be completed within 24 months by June 15, 2028.
The award is expected to contribute positively to the company's earnings and net assets per share for the financial year ending June 30, 2026, 2027 and 2028.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Timor-Leste's peace journey offers lessons in an uncertain world
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Timor-Leste's transformation from conflict to nation-building offers valuable lessons in peacebuilding, resilience and sustainable development, says Deputy Foreign Minister Datuk Lukanisman Awang Sauni.
He said nations that had successfully emerged from conflict and pursued lasting peace could provide important insights at a time when international stability was being tested by mounting geopolitical challenges.
"Timor-Leste's journey from struggle and uncertainty to stability and nation-building provides valuable lessons on both the challenges and opportunities in creating a more peaceful and resilient future," he said.
He was speaking at the book launch of "The Rising Sun: Timor-Leste's Journey to Peace and Development."
The event was held in conjunction with a distinguished lecture by Timor-Leste President and Nobel Peace Prize laureate Jose Ramos-Horta.
Also present were acting High Commissioner of Australia Simon Fellows, and Institute of Diplomacy and Foreign Relations director-general Datuk Zainol Rahim Zainuddin.
Lukanisman said Timor-Leste's experience underscored the importance of reconciliation, strong institutions, inclusive development and international cooperation in securing lasting peace.
He said Malaysia had remained a steadfast partner in Timor-Leste's development, from contributing to peacekeeping efforts following the 1999 referendum to becoming the first country to establish a liaison office and the first Asean member state to open an embassy in Dili.
Malaysia, he added, had also consistently supported Timor-Leste's aspiration to join Asean.
"The accession reflects not only the determination and resilience of the Timorese people, but also Asean's commitment to fostering a more inclusive and united Southeast Asia," he said.
Lukanisman stressed that peacebuilding was a shared responsibility requiring the participation of governments, academia, civil society, the private sector and local communities.
"Each stakeholder has a role in promoting understanding, strengthening trust and creating the conditions necessary for sustainable peace and development," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fahmi: J-Kom chief told to avoid letting govt messages be manipulated
Article:
KOTA BARU: Newly-appointed Community Communications Department (J-Kom) director-general Mohd Hisyamuddin Ghazali has been advised to ensure that information conveyed to the public is not open to manipulation by parties seeking to undermine the government.
Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the reminder came following Hisyamuddin's active and outspoken presence on social media since assuming the post last week.
At the same time, Fahmi said J-Kom must exercise caution to ensure the public clearly understands the messages and policies being communicated by the government.
"This is only his first week and we will continue to assess the quality of his service. At this point, he has received advice and I have also advised him personally.
"We must not create space that can be manipulated by parties who are hostile towards us and deliberately looking for issues to exploit.
"However, I see his speed in highlighting and addressing issues as something refreshing," he told reporters after presenting appointment letters to chairmen of the National Information Dissemination Centre (Nadi) state advisory panels.
Fahmi said the energy and dynamism demonstrated by Hisyamuddin were commendable, but stressed the need for caution to ensure government policies and aspirations were properly understood by the people.
"We must ensure that whatever is communicated helps the people understand the government's vision and direction," he said.
It was reported on June 13 that Hisyamuddin
came under fire
for making what was claimed to be a racially charged remark in a now-amended Facebook post.
Hisyamuddin was appointed J-KOM director-general on June 8, succeeding Datuk Ismail Yusop.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pas move to oust Bersatu from PN may backfire, says analyst
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pas risks losing support among moderate voters if the Islamist party decides to push for the removal of Bersatu from Perikatan Nasional, a political analyst said.
Universiti Malaya political analyst Associate Professor Dr Mohammad Tawfik Yaakub said while the Islamist party may gain greater control over the opposition's direction, it risks losing support from moderate voters and those more inclined towards national leadership figures than party identities.
"The situation could create space for the emergence of a new opposition bloc led by Bersatu, Parti Wawasan Negara under Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin, or other political alignments ahead of next general election.
"In the short term, Pas may become stronger organisationally, but in the longer term, the split could fragment opposition votes and ultimately benefit the unity government."
Tawfik said the implications of a complete Pas-Bersatu separation would extend beyond PN itself and could reshape the wider opposition landscape.
He said the opposition could evolve from a single major bloc into at least two competing camps, with Pas focusing on consolidating support among Malay-Muslim voters while seeking new political partners more aligned with its agenda.
His comments came ahead of a key PN leadership meeting scheduled for Wednesday, where coalition leaders are expected to discuss Bersatu's position within the opposition pact following Pas' decision to end political cooperation with the party.
Bersatu is, meanwhile, calling for an emergency meeting tonight ahead of the PN leadership meeting.
Meanwhile, Universiti Teknologi Malaysia Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities lecturer Associate Professor Dr Mazlan Ali said removing Bersatu from PN would not be straightforward as the coalition's constitution requires approval from its highest decision-making body.
He said Article 17A of the PN constitution stipulates that any decision involving disciplinary action or the removal of a component party must be approved by the coalition's Presidential Council.
"The Presidential Council consists of the presidents of Pas, Bersatu, Gerakan and MIPP. Any proposal to remove a component party requires majority support from the council.
"This means Pas cannot unilaterally expel Bersatu from PN. Likewise, Bersatu cannot remove Pas without securing the support of the other component parties."
Mazlan pointed to a recent proposal to admit another party into PN which failed after only Pas supported the move, while Bersatu opposed it.
"If Pas secures the backing of Gerakan and MIPP, then Bersatu could be removed from PN. But the decision must go through the proper constitutional process."
He said Bersatu's removal from PN would carry significant political consequences, including the loss of the coalition's logo and branding.
"Bersatu would no longer be able to contest under the PN banner and would have to use its own logo.
"If such a split occurs, it would also trigger a major realignment within the opposition, with both Pas and Bersatu pursuing separate political coalitions and strategies moving forward."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MRCB diversifies into AI digital infra with RM2.1bil data centre venture in Bukit Jalil
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian Resources Corporation Bhd (MRCB) has today announced its strategic entry into the digital infrastructure sector through a collaboration to develop a state-of-the-art, AI-ready data centre in Bukit Jalil.
Bukit Jalil Sentral Property Sdn Bhd, a wholly-owned subsidiary of MRCB Land Sdn Bhd,which in turn is a wholly-owned subsidiary of MRCB, signed a collaboration greement with Perintis Akal Sdn Bhd for the project.
The project carries an estimated gross development cost of RM2.1 billion.
Bukit Jalil Sentral, the owner of the 37,320-square-metre leasehold project land, will serve as an asset owner and the master developer of the proposed development.
Perintis Akal, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Pemandu Partners International PLT, will act as the long-term tenant and operator of the AI-ready data centre facility pursuant to a proposed 10-year lease agreement.
Designed to support the next generation of artificial intelligence, the facility will feature a total build-up area of approximately 500,000 square feet and a capacity of 65 MW IT load.
Built in accordance with strict Uptime Institute Tier III requirements, it will feature high-density power and cooling infrastructure specifically engineered to handle advanced GPU-based workloads and modern AI accelerator platforms.
To ensure world-class execution, Perintis Akal is partnering with Inspur Communication Malaysia Sdn Bhd for the construction, commissioning, maintenance, and operation of the facility.
Inspur Communication is part of the Inspur Group, a leading Chinese cloud computing and big data services provider.
"This collaboration represents a major milestone for MRCB, positioning the group at the forefront of the rapidly expanding high-performance computing and AI ecosystem.
"Beyond establishing a footprint in a high-growth sector, the project will ensure a steady stream of long-term recurring income via a 10-year lease structure, significantly enhancing the asset value of our land in Bukit Jalil.
"MRCB has already received multiple expressions of interest to offtake the 65 MW of IT load capacity, indicating further data centre development opportunities on the remainder of our Bukit Jalil land bank being a strong possibility," said Datuk Imran Salim, MRCB group managing director.
The parties expect to finalise and execute definitive agreements by the third quarter of 2026, with the entire facility targeted for completion by the fourth quarter of 2027.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 16, 2026 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia extended its recovery for a second consecutive session, ending higher as investor sentiment strengthened amid improved regional market conditions.
The benchmark index closed above the 1,700 level, settling at 1,709 points.
Among the most actively traded counters at the closing bell were Tanco Holdings, Pentech and Hong Seng Consolidated.
The ringgit ended slightly weaker against the US dollar, closing at 4.0600.
In the cryptocurrency market, Bitcoin climbed to RM271,199, while Ethereum also moved higher to RM7,289.
That's it for Market Pulse.

Title: RM3mil in illegal e-waste seized in Bukit Mertajam raid, 5 arrested
Article:
BUKIT MERTAJAM: Authorities seized an estimated RM3 million worth of electronic waste (e-waste) during a raid on an illegal processing and storage facility here yesterday.
The operation, dubbed Op Hazard (E-Waste), was carried out about 11.30am by police from the Seberang Prai Tengah police headquarters in collaboration with the Penang Department of Environment (DoE), Inland Revenue Board (IRB) and Seberang Prai City Council (MBSP).
Seberang Prai Tengah police chief Assistant Commissioner Nik Aminuddin Raja Abdullah said inspections revealed that the premises was being used as a storage and processing centre for e-waste materials.
"Inspection by the DoE uncovered 15 jumbo bags containing electrical circuit boards and electronic components believed to be scheduled waste, with an estimated value of RM3 million.
"Samples of the materials have been taken for analysis and verification in accordance with the Environmental Quality Act 1974," he said in a statement today.
The case is being investigated under Section 18(1) and Section 31 of the same Act.
Nik Aminuddin said initial investigations found that the premises had allegedly been operating as an e-waste storage and processing centre for about a year.
Authorities also discovered that the business did not have a valid operating licence.
MBSP subsequently issued two compounds for conducting business without a licence.
During the raid, police detained three Malaysians and two foreigners aged between 35 and 60.
"Preliminary investigations indicated that the three Malaysians were believed to be the owners and operators of the facility, while the two foreigners failed to produce valid work permits during inspections," said Nik Aminuddin.
Police are investigating the case under Section 55B of the Immigration Act 1959/63 for employing foreign workers without valid passes or work permits, while the foreigners are being investigated under Regulation 39(b) of the Immigration Regulations 1963 for breaching the conditions of their passes.
A remand order was obtained from the Bukit Mertajam magistrate's court, with the three Malaysian suspects remanded for a day, while the two foreigners were remanded for four days until June 19.
"We will continue strengthening strategic cooperation with enforcement agencies to curb illegal activities and ensure compliance with the country's laws and regulations," said Nik Aminuddin.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: As US nears birthday, poll shows many Americans doubt it will last another 250 years
Article:
WASHINGTON: As the US approaches its 250th birthday next month, two out of five Americans do not believe it will endure another 250 years beyond that, according to a Reuters/Ipsos poll that highlighted deep divisions over how the nation views itself.
The four-day poll, which concluded on Monday, comes amid the polarising pageantry that President Donald Trump has brought to celebrations for July 4, which will mark 250 years since the people who became known as the founding fathers of the US declared their independence from Britain.
Trump has put himself at the centre of many of the events to mark the anniversary, including staging a White House cage match on his birthday on Sunday.
On Monday he said he would be the main attraction at a July 4 celebration in Washington that will also serve as a political rally for the Republican as his party looks to keep control of Congress in November's midterm elections.
Trump has framed his presidency as a bid to save America from being destroyed by Democrats.
Democratic leaders contend it is Trump who is the danger to democracy and allege he is using federal law enforcement to target political critics.
Some 38 per cent of respondents in the poll - including 40 per cent of Democrats and 26 per cent of Republicans - said they didn't think the US will exist as a single country 250 years from now.
Just 62 per cent thought their nation would last.
Trump has accused Democrats – and especially the prior presidential administration of Democrat Joe Biden – of illegally targeting his allies, including those involved in the January 6, 2021, assault on the US Capitol, which was an attempt to overthrow Trump's loss in the 2020 presidential election.
Republicans also point to multiple assassination attempts against Trump as evidence that the leader's opponents are bent on violence.
Two-thirds of respondents – including 85 per cent of Democrats and 50 per cent of Republicans – said they agreed with a statement that American democracy was in danger of failing.
The overall share seeing democracy at risk was up from 57 per cent in a poll conducted in August of last year, with the increase driven by more Republicans worried about democracy's staying power.
Trump for years has claimed falsely that his 2020 loss was the result of widespread voter fraud and has been pushing for changes to voting laws.
Some 77 per cent of poll respondents said it was likely that political violence would increase in the next five years.
The poll also showed the share of Americans who see the country as a global standout is on the decline.
Some 30 per cent of respondents said they considered America the greatest country in the world, down from 38 per cent in a Reuters/Ipsos poll conducted in November 2017, during Trump's first term in office.
The share of Democrats with this view fell to 11 per cent from 26 per cent, while the share of Republicans held steady at about six in 10.
A majority of Americans – including three-quarters of Democrats and half of Republicans – said they thought the events celebrating the country's 250th anniversary had grown too political.
Americans were also divided along more mundane matters like how to celebrate Independence Day.
Some 52 per cent of Republicans said their celebrations would include wearing red, white and blue clothing - the colours of the US national flag - compared to 20 per cent of Democrats.
Republicans were more likely than Democrats to say they planned to attend a fireworks show – 46 to 28 per cent.
The poll, which was conducted online, gathered responses from 1,537 US adults nationwide and its results had a margin of error of three percentage points in either direction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: King extends condolences to families of soldiers killed in Gurun blast
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia extended his condolences to the families of two soldiers who were killed in an explosion during training at the Hobart Camp shooting range in Gurun, Kedah today.
In a Facebook post, Sultan Ibrahim said he hopes the families of the victims find strength and patience in this difficult time.
"Al-Fatihah for the two soldiers who perished in this tragedy. May they be placed among the faithful and the martyrs," the post said.
Sultan Ibrahim, the Malaysian Armed Forces Supreme Commander, also ordered the armed forces to conduct a thorough investigation into the incident.
The two soldiers killed in the 10.57am blast were Corporal Norazmi Abu Bakar of the 6th Battalion, Royal Malay Regiment, and Private Siti Khadijah Sungip of the 1st Squadron, Royal Army Engineers Regiment.
They were rushed to Sultan Abdul Halim Hospital in Sungai Petani but died on the way.
Police have classified the case as sudden death.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Asean must rethink approach to Myanmar conflict, says Timor-Leste president
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Asean's latest member Timor-Leste has called for the region to rethink its approach to the conflict in Myanmar, arguing that ending the fighting should take precedence over an immediate political settlement.
Timor-Leste President Dr Jose Ramos-Horta said efforts to restore democracy in Myanmar would remain futile unless the country's warring parties first agreed to a ceasefire.
"Step number one: stop the conflict.
"Step number two: stop the conflict, try to normalise the humanitarian situation, bring in NGOs, international agencies, neighbours to help with the humanitarian situation.
"Leave the political agenda for later," he said after giving a public lecture titled "Navigating Turbulent Times, Pursuing Peace".
Also present were Deputy Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Lukanisman Awang Sauni, acting Australian High Commissioner Simon Fellows, and Institute of Diplomacy and Foreign Relations director-general Datuk Zainol Rahim Zainuddin.
The Nobel Peace Prize laureate was responding to a question on growing anti-refugee sentiment in Malaysia, particularly against the Rohingya community, amid the prolonged conflict in Myanmar.
Drawing on decades of experience as a peace negotiator and mediator in conflicts from Southeast Asia to Africa and Latin America, Ramos-Horta argued that stability and humanitarian relief must come before political transition.
"Democracy can wait in Myanmar if just that the military and others agree that we must stop this," he said.
He said such a position may be controversial but maintained that peace processes around the world often require sequencing priorities rather than attempting to solve every issue simultaneously.
He described Myanmar as one of the most complicated conflicts in the world, noting that unlike other insurgencies, the country is home to numerous ethnic armed organisations with different interests and objectives.
"It is the only conflict in the world that I know of that you have like 18 armed groups," he said.
The complexity of the conflict, he added, explains why no regional or international actor has been able to broker a lasting settlement despite years of diplomatic efforts.
Ramos-Horta also suggested that asean may need to consider empowering a highly respected neutral figure to facilitate dialogue among Myanmar's stakeholders.
Citing asean's role in helping resolve the Cambodian conflict in the late 1980s and early 1990s, he said successful mediation often depends on individuals who can provide political cover and a face-saving exit for rival parties.
"It (peace) is possible. But it has to involve someone with high authority, maybe a four-star general, retired, from the region."
He added that the consequences of Myanmar's conflict extend far beyond its borders, placing mounting pressure on neighbouring countries dealing with refugee flows and humanitarian challenges.
He singled out the plight of the Rohingya, saying that any future settlement would be incomplete if the community continues to be excluded from discussions about Myanmar's future.
"You cannot resolve the problem of Myanmar by overlooking the Rohingya problem," he said.
Ramos-Horta said leaders in Naypyidaw should recognise that instability in Myanmar has become a regional burden.
"What you are doing, what you don't want to do, is a burden on your neighbours. It destabilises your neighbours.
"Someone in Myanmar holding power today might see the best is to make a grand gesture and unite all," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral: A cough, a slap and an apology
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: It is often said forgiveness is a virtue easier preached than practised  - especially when the lesson comes with an unexpected slap before breakfast.
For Ahmad Zhafri Samsudin, a simple queue for nasi lemak turned into an incident nobody saw coming, after a senior citizen allegedly took offence at his cough and the situation escalated into a heated confrontation.
Days after the incident went viral, Zhafri has found it in his heart to forgive the elderly man.
The victim shared the update on Facebook today, along with a video showing him shaking hands with the man, days after the incident went viral.
In the video, Zhafri was seen accepting the apology while advising the man not to repeat his actions and to learn from the incident.
He said the individual had surrendered himself at the Sungai Buloh district police headquarters after nearly three days, following efforts by police to locate him.
"With an open heart, I choose to forgive him. If Prophet Muhammad could forgive his people, who am I, with all my shortcomings, not to give someone room to correct his mistake?" he said in the post.
Zhafri said he chose not to retaliate, adding he believed in allowing the law to take its course.
"Perhaps this decision will not satisfy everyone, but as a son, husband, father, professional technologist and corporate sector worker, I believe action based on the law is better than responding in anger," he said.
Zhafri expressed hope the incident would serve as a lesson and not be repeated, but said he would not hesitate to take appropriate legal action if a similar incident occurred again.
A video of the incident went viral recently, showing an elderly man arguing with a younger man while both were queuing to buy nasi lemak for breakfast.
The incident was believed to have started after the younger man coughed, which allegedly angered the elderly man.
Media reports said the elderly man punched the younger man in the jaw, pushed him, slapped his face and hurled vulgar insults at him.
Following the incident, Sungai Buloh police issued a statement saying they were tracking down the suspect.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Israeli Supreme Court rejects appeal for release of Gaza doctor held without charge
Article:
TEL AVIV: Israel's Supreme Court on Tuesday rejected an appeal to release a prominent Palestinian doctor who has been held without charge since he was captured in Gaza in late 2024.
Hussam Abu Safiya, the director of the Kamal Adwan hospital, is among at least 14 doctors from Gaza who have been detained in Israel without charge for more than a year.
The court based its decision on "confidential materials" that were not shared with Abu Safiya or his lawyer, Naji Abbas, director of the Prisoners and Detainees Department at the Israeli rights organisation Physicians for Human Rights Israel (PHRI), told Reuters on Tuesday.
A spokesperson for the Supreme Court declined to comment.
"The message sent by this decision is unmistakable: a medical professional can be deprived of his liberty indefinitely without being charged and without the authorities presenting evidence against him in open court," Abbas said in a statement.
Abu Safiya's lawyer and human rights groups say the medic has been denied sufficient food and assaulted in prison.
Israel's prison service has denied the allegations. Abu Safiya appeared by video link at a Supreme Court hearing in Jerusalem last Wednesday, looking noticeably thinner.
For the past 13 days, Abu Safiya has also been held in solitary confinement, PHRI said.
The Israeli military has accused Abu Safiya of being a member of the Palestinian resistance group Hamas.
It has not provided evidence and Gaza's health ministry and Hamas have denied the allegation.
In 2023, Abu Safiya was among the doctors who refused to leave the dozens of newborn infants they were treating after the Israeli military ordered them to leave.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Judge: Najib’s lack of remorse, role in siphoning 1MDB money were factors when deciding his jail, RM13b sentence
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 16 — How did the High Court decide on how many years to imprison and how much to fine former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak in the RM2.2 billion 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) trial?
Trial judge Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah, who is now a Federal Court judge, today released his 809-page judgment that listed in detail why he had found Najib guilty of all 25 charges in the case.
Ultimately, Najib was ordered in the 1MDB trial to serve 15 years’ of jail time simultaneously; and was fined RM11.38 billion for power abuse, and fined a separate RM2.08 billion for money laundering.
Among other things, the judge said preventing crime would be an more important factor than Najib’s public service, and also said Najib had not shown remorse for his crimes.
Here’s Malay Mail’s quick summary of what the 1MDB trial judge considered when deciding on Najib’s sentence on December 26, 2025, based on the 809-page written decision:
The judge said “public interest” is the most important factor in deciding on the sentence, which includes deterring or discouraging others from committing crime, and also discouraging convicted offenders from doing crime again.
One key theme is the need for deterrence: “This is basically, the sending of a message out from the court that if you are prepared to do the crime, then be also prepared to pay the price for it by spending the time in prison.”
1MDB trial judge Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah said Najib was not a ‘humble employee’, but a person in exalted position who abused and betrayed the trust placed in him. — Picture by Yusof Isa
What factors did the judge consider?
1. The crime’s serious implications to society
Judge said 1MDB, which was founded in 2009 to promote Malaysia’s economic growth, instead became the “centre of one of the largest financial corruption and money laundering scandals in modern history”.
“The repercussions and consequences arising from the fallout of the scandal is still ongoing and there are current financial obligations that still need to be fulfilled. This will impact future generations of Malaysians as well,” the judge said, referring to the prime minister’s December 2, 2025 parliamentary remarks that around RM13 billion of 1MDB debt remains outstanding.
2. 1MDB financial scandal’s damage to Malaysia’s reputation
The judge said the complex web of financial transactions in the 1MDB case resulted in “colossal” financial losses, and “severe and enduring financial consequences” for Malaysia.
“It was described internationally as being ‘kleptocracy at its worst’, an unwanted blemish on the record of our beloved country’s otherwise sterling reputation on the world stage,” the judge said.
3. Najib’s abuse and betrayal of the trust given to him in his ‘exalted positions’
“At the heart of this scandal, stood not a humble employee of an organisation who saw an opportunity to exploit a flaw in the system, but a man exalted and who held the highest reins of power,” the judge said, referring to Najib.
The judge said Najib had simultaneously held three positions (prime minister, finance minister, 1MDB board of advisers’ chairman), used and exploited those positions to act and influence the 1MDB board “to carry out certain abnormal transactions with unholy haste”.
4. Najib’s previous conviction in the RM42 million SRC case, which involves a similar offence to that in the 1MDB tria
5. Najib’s nearly 47 years of public service and previous service as prime minister
But does Najib’s record of public service and contribution to the nation outweighs his breach of the trust given to him?
The judge said no, and stressed the more important need to discourage people from doing crime:
”After taking into account all these factors including the magnitude of the crime both in terms of the colossal sums involved and magnitude and scale of the repercussions from the scandal, including the ongoing financial obligations of the nation for the present and future generations, this court is of the view that a deterrent sentence is called for and this therefore outweighs the public service record and contribution to the service of the nation by the accused.”
6. Najib’s blaming of others for 1MDB scandal, lack of remorse
“This court has also to rightfully consider that the accused has not exhibited or displayed any remorse for the offences committed, having chosen instead to attach the blame for this scandal to others including the management of 1MDB.”
The judge instead said evidence in court clearly shown that Najib had orchestrated the siphoning of money from 1MDB through his proxy Low Taek Jho’s deception, and had worked “hand in hand” with and through Low to make a complex web of transactions which resulted in 1MDB funds eventually reaching Najib’s private bank account.
Before the judge decided on Najib’s sentence, his legal team had tried to argue that Najib did show remorse through his son’s public reading of his apology letter on October 24, 2024.
In that letter, Najib had reportedly apologised for the 1MDB debacle happening under his watch as prime minister, and said it is unfair for him to be held legally responsible and had hoped to be proved innocent.
7. Najib’s actions to cover up crimes when the 1MDB financial scandal first became public
The judge said Najib’s actions included disbanding a task force looking into the 1MDB scandal, and attempt to persuade then Bank Negara Malaysia’s governor to issue a statement to clear him of all wrongdoing.
After listing all these factors, the judge then decided on Najib’s jail sentence and fine in the 1MDB case.
What we know about Najib’s status now (SRC and 1MDB)
Najib is currently serving his reduced six-year jail term from the SRC case, with his imprisonment due to be completed in August 2028 or August 2029 (depending on whether he pays the RM50 million fine there).
Najib’s SRC jail term can end earlier if he shows good behaviour as a prisoner and his jail time is reduced by one-third.
The 1MDB trial judge has ordered that Najib immediately start serving his 15-year jail term, after his SRC jail time ends.
Najib has filed an appeal against his conviction and sentence in the 1MDB case, but has not applied for a stay or pause on his 1MDB sentence.
No hearing date has been fixed for Najib’s 1MDB appeal, as it is still in the preliminary stages. The appeal is scheduled for case management on June 19 at the Court of Appeal.

Title: Anwar to meet Putin in Kazan at Asean-Russia commemorative summit
Article:
KAZAN (Russia), June 16 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will lead the Malaysian delegation to the Asean-Russia Commemorative Summit in Kazan from June 17 to 18.
In a statement on Tuesday, Wisma Putra said Anwar will be accompanied by Minister of Investment, Trade and Industry Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani, Minister of Economy Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir, as well as officials from the Prime Minister’s Office and the Foreign Ministry.
The summit will mark the 35th anniversary of Asean-Russia relations, which were established in Kuala Lumpur in 1991.
According to the statement, the meeting will provide an opportunity for Asean and Russia to review progress achieved over the past 35 years and chart the future direction of cooperation under the Asean-Russia Strategic Partnership.
Discussions are expected to focus on strengthening practical cooperation in trade and investment, energy, food security, digital economy, science and technology, culture, education, tourism, and people-to-people exchanges.
Leaders are also expected to exchange views on regional and international developments.
The summit is expected to adopt four outcome documents, namely the Kazan Declaration on the 35th Anniversary of Asean-Russia Relations, the Joint Statement on Energy Cooperation, the Joint Statement on Cultural Cooperation, and the Comprehensive Plan of Action to Implement the Asean-Russia Strategic Partnership 2026-2030.
Wisma Putra said the documents will guide Asean-Russia cooperation in the coming years and support efforts to build a more resilient and future-oriented partnership.
“Malaysia’s participation reflects the country’s commitment to strengthening Asean’s external relations, promoting Asean Centrality, and advancing practical cooperation with Dialogue Partners in an increasingly complex global environment,” the statement said.
While in Kazan, Anwar is also expected to meet Russian President Vladimir Putin and the Rais of the Republic of Tatarstan, and hold engagements with Asean counterparts to discuss bilateral relations and issues of common concern. — Bernama

Title: Boy, 4, dies after being found unconscious at Sungai Buloh water theme park
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 16 — A four-year-old boy has died after being found weak and unconscious at a water theme park in Sungai Buloh on Sunday.
Sungai Buloh district police chief Supt Mohd Hafiz Muhammad Nor said initial investigations found that the victim is believed to have fallen after leaving the pool area.
"The victim was taken to Sungai Buloh Hospital for further treatment before being pronounced dead by medical officers,” he said in a statement today.
Mohd Hafiz said so far, investigations have not found any criminal elements, and further investigations are ongoing to determine the cause of death.
Members of the public with information related to the incident are urged to come forward to assist investigations by contacting the investigating officer, Insp Muhamad Zul Irfan Omar at 017-5393648 or any nearby police station.
He also advised the public not to speculate or spread unverified information regarding the incident until investigations are completed. — Bernama

Title: KL, Selangor, Sabah, Sarawak, and more: MetMalaysia’s thunderstorm warning hits most of Malaysia
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 16 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has triggered a thunderstorm alert for several states across Peninsular Malaysia, Sarawak, Sabah, and the Federal Territory of Labuan.
Residents are urged to prepare for heavy rainfall, strong winds, and lightning. The warning, issued at 5.15pm, stays active until 9.00pm.
Affected areas
Peninsular Malaysia:
Kedah: Pendang, Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling, Kulim, and Bandar Baharu
Perak: Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang, and Muallim
Kelantan: Jeli, Kuala Krai, and Gua Musang
Terengganu: Hulu Terengganu, Marang, Dungun, and Kemaman
Pahang: Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Raub, Bentong, Bera, and Rompin
Selangor: Hulu Selangor, Gombak, and Hulu Langat
Negeri Sembilan: Jelebu, Kuala Pilah, Jempol, and Tampin
Johor: Segamat, Kluang, and Mersing
Federal Territory: Kuala Lumpur
East Malaysia
Sarawak: Miri and Limbang
Sabah: Kuala Penyu (Interior), Ranau (West Coast), Tawau, Kunak, Lahad Datu (Tawau), and Kota Marudu (Kudat)
